blurb_en:
Meta launched a feature that let anyone use photos from public Instagram accounts to generate AI images — without alerting the person whose face was in them.
• The feature was part of Muse Image, Meta’s new AI image generator. Tagging any public account in a prompt put their photos in play.
• No notification was sent. The setup was opt-out, not opt-in — meaning your public profile was already enrolled by default.
• CAA — whose clients include Tom Hanks and Meryl Streep — contacted Meta directly. Public Citizen called it an egregious invasion of privacy.
• Meta removed the feature within days. The product team apparently didn’t model what it felt like to be on the receiving end.
• Instagram has roughly two billion users with public accounts. All of them were default-enrolled.
• The feature is gone. The question it raised — whether a public photo is fair game for AI — has not been answered.
blurb_zh:
Meta上线了一项功能，允许任何人用公开Instagram账号的照片生成AI图像——无需通知当事人。
• 该功能属于Meta全新AI图像生成器Muse Image的一部分：在提示词中@某公开账号，对方照片即成素材。
• 无需通知，默认参与——你的公开主页已在不知情的情况下被纳入范围。
• CAA（代理汤姆·汉克斯、梅丽尔·斯特里普等明星的经纪公司）直接向Meta提出抗议，公民组织Public Citizen称此举“严重侵犯隐私”。
• Meta在几天内将功能下线。产品团队显然没考虑过被使用者的感受。
• Instagram约20亿公开账号用户，全部被默认纳入。
• 功能没了，但问题还在：公开的照片，真的属于任何人吗？
verification_notes: Confirmed via Variety, TechCrunch snippet, Axios report, The Hill — four consistent credible sources. Feature confirmed removed July 10.

blurb_en:
The company that turned text prompts into paintings has announced it is building a full-body medical scanner — and the prototype involves descending into a pool of water surrounded by 358,000 ultrasound transducers.
• Midjourney calls the device an Ultrasonic CT scanner. It captures a complete three-dimensional map of your organs in about 60 seconds, without radiation.
• The technology is licensed from Butterfly Network. Founder David Holz says this is the first new whole-body imaging method in 50 years.
• Midjourney is committing $74 million to the project across a five-year licensing deal.
• The first installation will be inside the Midjourney Spa — a 25,000-square-foot San Francisco space with hot tubs, saunas, cold plunges, and a whole-body CT scanner.
• The device has no FDA diagnostic clearance. Futurism published a piece headlined “We Are Extremely Skeptical.” Both things can be true simultaneously.
• For a camera-and-imaging company: the question isn’t what AI images look like anymore — it’s what your internal organs are worth at scale.
blurb_zh:
这家把文字变成图画的公司宣布，正在研发一款全身医学扫描仪——原型机需要你沉入水池，周身环绕着35.8万个超声波换能器。
• Midjourney称该设备为"超声CT扫描仪"，可在约60秒内无辐射完成全身三维器官成像。
• 技术来自Butterfly Network授权。创始人David Holz表示，这是50年来首创的全新全身成像方法。
• Midjourney为此签署五年授权协议，投入7400万美元。
• 首个落地场景是旧金山"Midjourney Spa"——2.3万平方英尺，配备热水浴缸、桑拿、冷浸池，以及这台全身CT扫描仪。
• 设备尚未获得FDA诊断许可。Futurism专门发文，标题是《我们极度怀疑》。两件事可以同时成立。
• 对影像行业而言，问题已不再是AI图像能有多逼真——而是你的内脏器官，批量扫描之后值多少钱。
verification_notes: Confirmed via official Midjourney Medical page, PYMNTS report, Forbes June 19 piece, radiologybusiness.com (SEC filing detail for $74M/Butterfly Network deal). Story is 33 days old but included as exceptional exception per editorial guidelines — directly relevant to Insta360 imaging audience.

blurb_en:
Spotify confirmed this week it removed more than 75 million tracks over the past year — nearly all of them flagged as AI-generated fraud.
• The platform receives around 100,000 new song uploads every day. A growing share are AI-generated, many placed there specifically to generate royalty payments.
• The deleted tracks share a profile: mass-uploaded under shell artist accounts, minimal metadata variation, run times just over 30 seconds — the streaming royalty threshold.
• Spotify is not banning AI music. It is targeting fraud: fake artists, unauthorized voice clones, and tracks engineered to game the payout system.
• New policies announced alongside the purge: crackdowns on SEO manipulation, excessive duplicate uploads, and sub-31-second gaming tracks.
• Deezer reported a similar problem in May, when it revealed 44% of its uploads were AI-generated. 75 million is a different number.
• Whether that’s a lot or just the ones that got caught depends on how optimistic you are.
blurb_zh:
Spotify本周证实，过去一年内已从平台删除超7500万首曲目——几乎全部被认定为AI生成的欺诈内容。
• 目前每天约10万首新歌上传至Spotify，AI生成的比例持续攀升，许多作品专门为套取版税而生。
• 被删曲目特征高度一致：以虚假艺人账号批量上传，元数据几乎雷同，时长刚过30秒——流媒体版税起算门槛。
• Spotify并未禁止AI音乐，而是打击欺诈：虚假艺人、未授权声音克隆、专为钻版税空子设计的音轨。
• 本次清理同步推出新政策：打击搜索引擎优化操纵、过度重复上传及刻意卡时长的"版税歌"。
• 今年5月，Deezer曾披露其44%的新上传内容为AI生成。7500万是另一个量级的数字。
• 这算多，还是只是被抓到的部分？取决于你有多乐观。
verification_notes: Original reporting by Music Business Worldwide (paywalled). Confirmed via Neowin, Gadget Review, Yahoo Tech, The Riff Report (dated 18/07/2026). All sources consistent on 75M+ figure and new policies.

blurb_en:
Kuaishou’s Kling AI closed a $2.8 billion funding round at a $15 billion valuation this month, backed by Alibaba, Tencent, and Baidu simultaneously — all three of China’s major tech platforms investing in a competitor to their own models.
• Kling’s annualized revenue reached $500 million in March 2026, up from $100 million in March 2025. It currently leads the text-to-video arena leaderboard.
• Tencent contributed $200 million despite operating Hunyuan, its own competing video model. The logic: Kling’s commercial traction is worth more than whatever advantage keeping it unfunded might provide.
• The round arrived roughly two months after OpenAI discontinued Sora’s consumer app, narrowing the competitive field.
• ByteDance’s Seedance 2.5 also launched this month with native 30-second video generation. China’s video AI market is consolidating quickly and funding heavily.
• $2.8 billion for a single AI video company requires a recalibration of what the entire sector is worth.
• Kling’s valuation now exceeds Runway’s last disclosed figure by a wide margin.
blurb_zh:
快手旗下可灵AI本月完成28亿美元融资，估值达150亿美元——阿里、腾讯、百度三大科技巨头同时入局，集体押注一个与自家产品存在竞争关系的视频AI平台。
• 可灵2026年3月年化收入达5亿美元，较2025年3月的1亿美元暴增五倍。目前稳居文生视频竞技榜首位。
• 腾讯投入2亿美元，尽管自家混元也有视频生成能力。逻辑是：可灵的商业吸引力，高过"饿死对手"的战略价值。
• 此轮融资距OpenAI关闭Sora消费端已过去约两个月，竞争格局明显收窄。
• 字节跳动的即梦2.5也于本月上线，支持原生30秒视频生成。中国AI视频市场正在加速整合与大规模融资。
• 单一AI视频公司拿下28亿美元，是需要重新校准整个赛道估值的那种数字。
• 可灵当前估值，已大幅超越Runway最近一次公开的估值。
verification_notes: Confirmed via CNBC (July 3, Kuaishou SEC filing data), TechFundingNews, arturmarkus.com analysis (ARR data). Story is 18 days old but included as significant exception — largest-ever AI video funding round, directly relevant to focus on Chinese AI industry.

blurb_en:
Google updated its Workspace video tool Vids this week so you can upload a selfie and a voice recording and have a digital version of yourself present on screen — no camera time required.
• You type what you want to say; the avatar delivers it. Meetings, presentations, and training videos without actually appearing in them.
• Google also added Gemini Omni to Vids, enabling full video sequences from text prompts and reference images — and phone footage background swaps and lighting fixes.
• Avatars are watermarked invisibly with SynthID and tied to the creator’s Google account. Access is limited to users aged 18 and over in supported regions.
• Available to Google Workspace business customers and AI Pro/Ultra subscribers.
• This is HeyGen’s market. Google’s distribution is not a small complication for HeyGen.
• The long-term question for camera-adjacent products: what’s the pitch when the output doesn’t require a camera?
blurb_zh:
谷歌本周更新了Workspace视频工具Vids，用户只需上传一张自拍和一段语音录音，即可生成自己的数字分身出镜——全程无需对着摄像头。
• 你只管打字输入内容，数字分身在屏幕上代替你说话。会议、演示、培训视频，全程不用露脸。
• 谷歌同时将Gemini Omni接入Vids：支持根据文字提示和参考图生成完整视频片段，还能替换手机录像的背景或修正光线。
• 数字分身由SynthID隐形水印保护，绑定至创作者的谷歌账号，仅限特定地区18岁以上用户使用。
• 目前向Google Workspace企业用户及AI Pro/Ultra订阅者开放。
• 这是HeyGen的地盘。谷歌的用户体量，对HeyGen来说不是小麻烦。
• 对相机及周边产品而言，长期的问题是：当输出内容不需要相机时，卖点在哪里？
verification_notes: Primary source: official Google Blog post July 16. Cross-confirmed via TechCrunch snippet, winbuzzer.com (July 20), Eastern Herald, lumienai.com — all consistent on selfie-to-avatar feature and Gemini Omni integration.

blurb_en:
Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act on July 6, creating what state officials are describing as the strongest AI safety law in the United States.
• The law applies only to the largest models: those generating more than $500 million in annual revenue, requiring annual third-party safety audits.
• Companies must publicly disclose how they identify catastrophic risk and report significant safety incidents to the state within 72 hours.
• Civil penalties: $1 million for a first violation, $3 million for each subsequent one.
• Illinois, California, and New York together account for roughly 40% of the US AI market, meaning the three states’ laws function as a de facto national standard in the absence of federal regulation.
• The law takes effect January 1, 2028, giving companies time to either comply or find creative interpretations of where they are incorporated.
• For context: the highest grade any frontier AI lab earned on the Future of Life Institute’s 2026 AI Safety Index was a C+.
blurb_zh:
伊利诺伊州州长普里茨克于7月6日签署《人工智能安全措施法》，州官员称其为全美目前最严苛的AI安全法律。
• 法律仅针对最大体量的模型：年收入超5亿美元的AI系统须接受年度第三方安全审计。
• 企业须公开披露如何识别"灾难性风险"，并在72小时内向州政府上报重大安全事件。
• 违规罚款：首次100万美元，此后每次300万美元。
• 伊利诺伊、加州和纽约三州合计约40%的美国AI市场，三州法规实际上已形成联邦级标准。
• 法律于2028年1月1日生效，给各公司留出时间：合规，或者想办法重新解释自己的注册地。
• 顺带一提：2026年未来生命研究所AI安全指数中，前沿AI实验室的最高评级是C+。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Capitol News Illinois, WTTW Chicago, Governor's official press release, Transparency Coalition, NPR Illinois, JURIST. Law signed July 6, 2026.

blurb_en:
TikTok is testing an opt-in tool that scans the platform for AI-generated deepfakes of creators' likenesses — and the price of using it is submitting a government ID and a biometric selfie to TikTok.
• The test is limited to a small group of US creators. To enroll, you verify your identity through Jumio: a real-time selfie plus a government-issued document.
• TikTok says it does not retain ID documents and does not store facial data after verification is complete.
• Once enrolled, the tool monitors TikTok for content mimicking your likeness. Confirmed matches can be reported for review and removal.
• TikTok has already automatically labeled more than 3 billion AI-generated videos on its platform.
• YouTube launched its own creator likeness detection program in October 2025. TikTok appears to be running the same play.
• So: to protect your face from AI impersonation, you hand a high-resolution scan of that face to a short-video app that previously faced bans over data-sharing concerns.
blurb_zh:
TikTok正在测试一项创作者可选加入的工具，用于扫描平台上以AI仿制创作者面孔的深伪内容——使用代价是向TikTok提交政府身份证件和实时生物特征自拍。
• 测试目前仅限美国部分创作者，注册需通过第三方身份核验机构Jumio：实时自拍加有效证件。
• TikTok表示不保存身份证件，验证完成后不留存面部数据。
• 完成注册后，工具将持续监测平台上模仿你面貌的内容，可举报并申请删除。
• TikTok目前已自动为超30亿条AI生成视频打上标签。
• YouTube于2025年10月上线了类似的创作者肖像保护功能。TikTok正在执行同一套打法。
• 结论：为了保护你的脸不被AI盗用，你要先把自己的脸和证件交给一个曾因数据安全问题面临禁令的短视频平台。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Digital Music News (July 20), Digital Trends, dataconomy.com, itechpost.com (July 18), techbriefly.com (July 20). TikTok spokesperson Zachary Kizer confirmed details to The Verge.
